  | {
      type: "is-copilot-api-available-updated";
    }
  | {
      type: "implement-todo";
      fileName: string;
      /** 1-based line number where the CodeLens was shown */
      line: number;
      /** The contents of the TODO/Codex comment on the line */
      comment: string;
    }
  | FetchResponse
  | {
      type: "window-fullscreen-changed";
      isFullScreen: boolean;
    }
  | {
      type: "app-update-ready-changed";
      isUpdateReady: boolean;
    }
  | {
      type: "electron-window-focus-changed";
      isFocused: boolean;
    }
  | FetchStreamEvent
  | FetchStreamError
  | FetchStreamComplete
  | {
      type: "add-context-file";
      file: FileDescriptor;
    }
  | {
      type: "desktop-notification-action";
      hostId: string;
      notificationId: string;
      actionId: string | nu…5787 tokens truncated…-telemetry-user";
      authMethod: ExtendedAuthMethod | null;
      userId: string | null;
      email: string | null;
    }
  | WorkerMessageFromView;

/**
 * vscode://codex/ requests from a webview that are written like normal fetch requests but handled by the extension
 * instead of making a real fetch network request.
 * Handled by extension-fetch-handler.ts
 */
export type ChildProcessKind =
  | "app_server"
  | "electron_gpu"
  | "electron_network"
  | "electron_proxy"
  | "electron_renderer"
  | "electron_utility"
  | "git"
  | "mcp"
  | "shell"
  | "other";

export type ChildProcessInfo = {
  pid: number;
  parentPid: number;
  depth: number;
  rootChildPid: number;
  kind: ChildProcessKind;
  command: string;
  ageSeconds: number | null;
  rssKb: number | null;
};

export type HotkeyWindowHotkeyState = {
  supported: boolean;
  configuredHotkey: string | null;
  isGateEnabled: boolean;
  isDevMode: boolean;
  isDevOverrideEnabled: boolean;
  /**
   * True only when runtime registration succeeded.
   * This is intentionally separate from config fields because shortcut
   * registration can still fail at runtime (for example, when reserved by OS).
   */
  isActive: boolean;
};

export type HotkeyWindowHotkeyMutationResponse =
  | {
      success: true;
      state: HotkeyWindowHotkeyState;
    }
  | {
      success: false;
      error: string;
      state: HotkeyWindowHotkeyState;
    };

export type FastModeRolloutMetricsParams = {
  endTimeMs?: number;
  maxRollouts?: number | null;
  startTimeMs: number;
};

export type FastModeRolloutMetrics = {
  completedTurnCount: number;
  endTimeMs: number;
  estimatedFastMs: number;
  estimatedSavedMs: number;
  estimatedStandardMs: number;
  estimatedTurnCount: number;
  maxRollouts: number | null;
  observedCompletedTurnWallTimeMs: number;
  parsedRolloutCount: number;
  rolloutCountWithCompletedTurns: number;
  rolloutCountWithEstimatedTurns: number;
  scannedRolloutCount: number;
  startTimeMs: number;
  totalGeneratedTokens: number;
  totalOutputTokens: number;
  totalReasoningOutputTokens: number;
};

export type VSCodeFetchRequest = {
  "active-workspace-roots": {
    request: undefined;
    response: {
      roots: Array<string>;
    };
  };
  "workspace-root-options": {
    request: undefined;
    response: {
      roots: Array<string>;
      labels?: Record<string, string>;
    };
  };
  "add-workspace-root-option": {
    request: {
      root: string;
      label?: string;
      setActive?: boolean;
    };
    response: { success: boolean };
  };
  "electron-clone-workspace-repo": {
    request: {
      url: string;
      dialogTitle: string;
    };
    response: { success: boolean; canceled: boolean; error: string | null };
  };
  "codex-home": {
    request: undefined;
    response: {
      codexHome: string;
      worktreesSegment: string;
    };
  };
  "app-connect-oauth-callback-url": {
    request: undefined;
    response: {
      callbackUrl: string;
    };
  };
  "fast-mode-rollout-metrics": {
    request: FastModeRolloutMetricsParams;
    response: FastModeRolloutMetrics | null;
  };
  "refresh-remote-ssh-connections": {
    request: undefined;
    response: {
      remoteConnections: Array<RemoteSshConnection>;
    };
  };
  "save-codex-managed-remote-ssh-connections": {
    request: {
      remoteConnections: Array<RemoteSshConnection>;
    };
    response: {
      remoteConnections: Array<RemoteSshConnection>;
    };
  };
  "set-remote-ssh-connection-auto-connect": {
    request: {
      hostId: string;
      autoConnect: boolean;
    };
    response: {
      remoteConnections: Array<RemoteSshConnection>;
      state: AppServerConnectionState | null;
      errorMessage: string | null;
    };
  };
  "start-remote-chatgpt-login-port-forward": {
    request: {
      hostId: string;
      loginId: string;
    };
    response: {
      forwarded: boolean;
    };
  };
  "stop-remote-chatgpt-login-port-forward": {
    request: {
      hostId: string;
      loginId: string;
    };
    response: {
      stopped: boolean;
    };
  };
  "app-server-connection-state": {
    request: {
      hostId: string;
    };
    response: {
      state: AppServerConnectionState | null;
      errorMessage: string | null;
    };
  };
  "remote-workspace-directory-entries": {
    request: {
      hostId: string;
      directoryPath?: string | null;
      directoriesOnly?: boolean;
    };
    response: {
      directoryPath: string;
      parentPath: string | null;
      entries: Array<{
        name: string;
        path: string;
        type: "directory" | "file";
      }>;
    };
  };
  "account-info": {
    request: undefined;
    response:
      | {
          accountId: string;
          userId: string;
          plan: string;
          email: string | null;
        }
      | {
          accountId: null;
          userId: null;
          plan: null;
          email: null;
        };
  };
  "extension-info": {
    request: undefined;
    response: {
      version: string;
      buildNumber: string | null;
      buildFlavor: BuildFlavor | null;
    };
  };
  "third-party-notices": {
    request: undefined;
    response: {
      text: string | null;
    };
  };
  "locale-info": {
    request: undefined;
    response: {
      ideLocale: string;
      systemLocale: string;
    };
  };
  "os-info": {
    request: undefined;
    response: {
      platform: string;
      hasWsl: boolean;
      isVsCodeRunningInsideWsl: boolean;
    };
  };
  "child-processes": {
    request: undefined;
    response: {
      processes: Array<ChildProcessInfo>;
    };
  };
  "hotkey-window-hotkey-state": {
    request: undefined;
    response: HotkeyWindowHotkeyState;
  };
  "hotkey-window-set-hotkey": {
    request: {
      hotkey: string | null;
    };
    response: HotkeyWindowHotkeyMutationResponse;
  };
  "hotkey-window-set-dev-hotkey-override": {
    request: {
      enabled: boolean;
    };
    response: HotkeyWindowHotkeyMutationResponse;
  };
  "feedback-create-sentry-issue": {
    request: {
      classification: string;
      description: string;
      includeLogs: boolean;
      correlationId: string;
    };
    response: {
      reportId: string;
    };
  };
  "openai-api-key": {
    request: undefined;
    response: {
      value: string | null;
    };
  };
  "recommended-skills": {
    request: {
      refresh?: boolean;
    };
    response: RecommendedSkillsResponse;
  };
  "local-custom-agents": {
    request: {
      roots?: Array<string>;
    };
    response: LocalCustomAgentsResponse;
  };
  "install-recommended-skill": {
    request: {
      skillId: string;
      repoPath: string;
      installRoot: string | null;
    };
    response: {
      success: boolean;
      destination: string | null;
      error: string | null;
    };
  };
  "remove-skill": {
    request: {
      skillPath: string;
    };
    response: {
      success: boolean;
      deletedPath: string | null;
      error: string | null;
    };
  };
  "read-file": {
    request: {
      path: string;
    };
    response: {
      contents: string;
    };
  };
  "read-file-binary": {
    request: {
      path: string;
    };
    response: {
      contentsBase64: string;
    };
  };
  "read-git-file-binary": {
    request: {
      cwd: GitCwd | null;
      path: string;
      ref: "head" | "index";
    };
    response: {
      contentsBase64: string | null;
    };
  };
  "codex-agents-md": {
    request: undefined;
    response: {
      path: string;
      contents: string;
    };
  };
  "codex-agents-md-save": {
    request: {
      contents: string;
    };
    response: {
      path: string;
    };
  };
  "generate-thread-title": {
    request: {
      hostId: string;
      prompt: string;
      cwd?: string | null;
    };
    response: {
      title: string | null;
    };
  };
  "generate-commit-message": {
    request: {
      hostId: string;
      prompt: string;
      cwd?: string | null;
      conversationId?: string | null;
      model: string;
    };
    response: {
      message: string | null;
    };
  };
  "generate-pull-request-message": {
    request: {
      hostId: string;
      prompt: string;
      cwd?: string | null;
      conversationId?: string | null;
      model: string;
    };
    response: {
      title: string | null;
      body: string | null;
    };
  };
  "inbox-items": {
    request: {
      limit?: number;
    };
    response: {
      items: Array<InboxItem>;
    };
  };
  "list-automations": {
    request: undefined;
    response: {
      items: Array<Automation>;
    };
  };
  "developer-instructions": {
    request: {
      baseInstructions?: string | null;
    };
    response: {
      instructions: string;
    };
  };
  "automation-create": {
    request: Pick<
      Automation,
      | "name"
      | "prompt"
      | "cwds"
      | "executionEnvironment"
      | "model"
      | "reasoningEffort"
      | "rrule"
    >;
    response: {
      item: Automation;
    };
  };
  "automation-update": {
    request: Pick<
      Automation,
      | "id"
      | "name"
      | "prompt"
      | "status"
      | "cwds"
      | "executionEnvironment"
      | "model"
      | "reasoningEffort"
      | "rrule"
    >;
    response: {
      item: Automation;
    };
  };
  "automation-delete": {
    request: Pick<Automation, "id">;
    response: {
      success: boolean;
    };
  };
  "automation-run-now": {
    request: Pick<Automation, "id">;
    response: {
      success: boolean;
    };
  };
  "list-pending-automation-run-threads": {
    /** Thread ids for automation runs that are pending acceptance. */
    request: undefined;
    response: {
      threadIds: Array<string>;
    };
  };
  "automation-run-archive": {
    request: {
      threadId: string;
      archivedAssistantMessage?: string | null;
      archivedUserMessage?: string | null;
      archivedReason?: AutomationArchiveReason;
    };
    response: {
      success: boolean;
    };
  };
  "automation-run-delete": {
    request: {
      threadId: string;
    };
    response: {
      success: boolean;
    };
  };
  "list-pinned-threads": {
    request: undefined;
    response: {
      threadIds: Array<string>;
    };
  };
  "set-thread-pinned": {
    request: {
      threadId: string;
      pinned: boolean;
    };
    response: {
      success: boolean;
    };
  };
  "set-pinned-threads-order": {
    request: {
      threadIds: Array<string>;
    };
    response: {
      success: boolean;
    };
  };
  "has-custom-cli-executable": {
    request: undefined;
    response: {
      hasCustomCliExecutable: boolean;
    };
  };
  "find-files": {
    request: {
      query: string;
      cwd?: string | null;
    };
    response: {
      files: Array<FileDescriptor>;
    };
  };
  "paths-exist": {
    request: {
      paths: Array<string>;
    };
    response: {
      existingPaths: Array<string>;
    };
  };
  "local-environment": {
    request: {
      configPath: string;
    };
    response: {
      environment: LocalEnvironmentResultWithPath;
    };
  };
  "local-environments": {
    request: {
      workspaceRoot: string;
    };
    response: {
      environments: Array<LocalEnvironmentResultWithPath>;
    };
  };
  "local-environment-config": {
    request: {
      configPath: string;
    };
    response: {
      configPath: string;
      exists: boolean;
      raw: string | null;
    };
  };
  "local-environment-config-save": {
    request: {
      configPath: string;
      raw: string;
    };
    response: {
      configPath: string;
      success: boolean;
    };
  };
  "ide-context": {
    request:
      | {
          // Required for Electron to determine which IDE workspace to connect to.
          workspaceRoot: string;
        }
      // Not required for VS Code.
      | undefined;
    response: {
      ideContext: IdeContext;
    };
  };
  "ipc-request": {
    request: {
      method: keyof IpcRequestMessageContent;
      params: IpcRequestMessageContent[keyof IpcRequestMessageContent]["params"];
      targetClientId?: string;
    };
    response: IpcResponseMessage;
  };
  "apply-patch": {
    request: ApplyPatchRequest & { hostConfig: HostConfig };
    response: ApplyPatchResult;
  };
  "open-in-targets": {
    request: { cwd: GitCwd | null };
    response: {
      preferredTarget?: OpenInTarget | null;
      availableTargets: Array<OpenInTarget>;
      targets: Array<{
        id: OpenInTarget;
        label: string;
        icon: string;
        default?: boolean;
        available?: boolean;
      }>;
    };
  };
  "set-preferred-app": {
    request: { target: OpenInTarget };
    response: { success: boolean };
  };
  "terminal-shell-options": {
    request: undefined;
    response: {
      availableShells: Array<
        ConfigValueByKey[typeof ConfigurationKeys.INTEGRATED_TERMINAL_SHELL]
      >;
    };
  };
  "thread-terminal-snapshot": {
    request: {
      threadId: string;
    };
    response: {
      session: {
        cwd: string;
        shell: string;
        buffer: string;
        truncated: boolean;
      } | null;
    };
  };
  "pick-file": {
    request: {
      pickerTitle: string;
    };
    response: {
      file: FileDescriptor | null;
    };
  };
  "pick-files": {
    request: {
      pickerTitle: string;
    };
    response: {
      files: Array<FileDescriptor>;
    };
  };
  "open-file": {
    request: {
      /**
       * Supported path formats (resolved against the current VS Code workspace):
       * - Absolute filesystem path (POSIX or Windows), e.g. "/Users/alex/src/app.ts" or "C:\\repo\\app.ts".
       * - Workspace-relative path using "/" or "\\", e.g. "src/app.ts".
       * - Git diff paths prefixed with "a/" or "b/" (these prefixes are ignored), e.g. "a/src/app.ts".
       * - Paths that include the workspace folder name as a leading segment, e.g. "my-repo/src/app.ts".
       * - Bare filename or suffix path; a best-effort search will open the first match, e.g. "app.ts" or "src/app.ts".
       *
       * Notes:
       * - This field expects a filesystem path string, not a URI. Schemes like "file://", "vscode://", or "https://" are not accepted.
       */
      path: string;
      /** Optional preferred target; when omitted, the host chooses the preferred target. */
      target?: OpenInTarget | null;
      /** 1-based line number to select (optional). */
      line?: number;
      /** 1-based column number to select (optional, defaults to 1). */
      column?: number;
      /** Base directory to resolve relative paths against; pass null when unavailable. */
      cwd: GitCwd | null;
      /**
       * When this and target are provided, this target will be persisted as the preferred target for this path.
       */
      persistPreferredTargetPath?: string;
    };
    response: { success: boolean };
  };
  "git-origins": {
    request:
      | undefined
      | {
          /** Optional host id to resolve git metadata against. */
          hostId?: string;
          /**
           * Optional list of directories to resolve git origins for.
           * When omitted, the workspace root options are used.
           */
          dirs?: Array<string>;
        };
    response: {
      origins: Array<{
        /**
         * If dirs were requested, this will be the directory that was requested.
         * If not, it will be one of the workspace roots.
         * Either is either the same or a subdirectory of the root.
         */
        dir: string;
        root: GitRoot;
        originUrl: string | null;
        commonDir?: string | null;
      }>;
      homeDir: string;
    };
  };
  "git-push": {
    request: GitPushRequest;
    response: GitPushResponse;
  };
  "git-create-branch": {
    request: GitCreateBranchRequest;
    response: GitCreateBranchResponse;
  };
  "git-checkout-branch": {
    request: GitCheckoutBranchRequest;
    response: GitCheckoutBranchResponse;
  };
  "gh-cli-status": {
    request:
      | undefined
      | {
          hostId?: string;
        };
    response: GhCliStatus;
  };
  "gh-pr-create": {
    request: GhCreatePullRequestRequest;
    response: GhCreatePullRequestResponse;
  };
  "gh-pr-board": {
    request: GhPullRequestBoardRequest;
    response: GhPullRequestBoardResponse;
  };
  "gh-pr-status": {
    request: GhPullRequestStatusRequest;
    response: GhPullRequestStatusResponse;
  };
  "gh-pr-merge": {
    request: GhMergePullRequestRequest;
    response: GhMergePullRequestResponse;
  };
  /** Returns the merge-base commit hash between HEAD and the provided base branch. */
  "git-merge-base": {
    request: {
      gitRoot?: string;
      baseBranch: string;
      hostId?: string;
    };
    response: {
      mergeBaseSha: string | null;
    };
  };
  "prepare-worktree-snapshot": {
    request: {
      gitRoot: string;
      snapshotBranch: string | null;
    };
    response: {
      tarballPath: string;
      tarballFilename: string;
      tarballSize: number;
      repoName: string;
      branch: string | null;
      snapshotBranch: string;
      commitSha: string;
      remotes: Record<string, string>;
      contentType: string;
    };
  };
  "upload-worktree-snapshot": {
    request: {
      tarballPath: string;
      uploadUrl: string;
      contentLength: number;
      contentType: string;
    };
    response: { success: boolean };
  };
  "confirm-trace-recording-start": {
    request: undefined;
    response: { success: boolean };
  };
  "cancel-trace-recording-start": {
    request: undefined;
    response: { success: boolean };
  };
  "submit-trace-recording-details": {
    request: {
      note: string;
      conversationId: string | null;
    };
    response: { success: boolean };
  };
  "get-configuration": {
    request: {
      key: (typeof ConfigurationKeys)[keyof typeof ConfigurationKeys];
    };
    response: {
      value: unknown;
    };
  };
  "set-configuration": {
    request: {
      key: ConfigurationKey;
      value: ConfigValueByKey[ConfigurationKey];
    };
    response: { success: boolean };
  };
  "get-global-state": {
    request: {
      key: (typeof GlobalStateKey)[keyof typeof GlobalStateKey];
    };
    response: {
      value: unknown;
    };
  };
  "set-global-state": {
    request: {
      key: (typeof GlobalStateKey)[keyof typeof GlobalStateKey];
      value: unknown;
    };
    response: { success: boolean };
  };
  /**
   * Set a VS Code context key in the workbench. Useful for keybindings/menus.
   */
  "set-vs-context": {
    request: {
      key: string;
      value?: string | boolean;
    };
    response: { success: boolean };
  };
  "get-copilot-api-proxy-info": {
    request: undefined;
    response: {
      baseUrl: string;
      /** Value of the API key. */
      secret: string;
    } | null;
  };
  "mcp-codex-config": {
    request: undefined;
    response: {
      config: CodexConfig | null;
    };
  };
  "worktree-shell-environment-config": {
    request: {
      cwd: string;
    };
    response: {
      shellEnvironment: {
        version: 1;
        set: Record<string, string>;
        exclude: Array<string>;
      } | null;
    };
  };
  "is-copilot-api-available": {
    request: undefined;
    response: {
      available: boolean;
    };
  };
};

// This enforces that all of the VSCodeFetchRequest and VSCodeMutationRequest keys have a request and response.
type AssertSubtype<_Sub extends Super, Super> = true;
type BaseVSCodeFetchRequest = {
  request: unknown;
  response: unknown;
};
type _AssertAllVSCodeRequestsHaveRequestAndResponse = AssertSubtype<
  VSCodeFetchRequest,
  Record<string, BaseVSCodeFetchRequest>
>;
type _AssertHotkeyWindowMutationStateMatchesQueryResponse = AssertSubtype<
  HotkeyWindowHotkeyMutationResponse["state"],
  VSCodeFetchRequest["hotkey-window-hotkey-state"]["response"]
>;
declare const _useAssertRequest: _AssertAllVSCodeRequestsHaveRequestAndResponse;
declare const _useAssertHotkeyWindowMutationStateMatchesQueryResponse: _AssertHotkeyWindowMutationStateMatchesQueryResponse;

C:\Users\ai\.vscode\extensions\openai.chatgpt-26.323.20928-win32-x64\webview\assets\initial-route-atom-_jj2dzad.js.map:1:{"version":3,"file":"initial-route-atom-_jj2dzad.js","names":["getInitialRoute","meta","document","querySelector","HTMLMetaElement","content","trim","searchRoute","URL","window","location","href","searchParams","get","initialRoute"],"sources":["../../src/initial-route-atom.ts"],"sourcesContent":["function getInitialRoute(): string | null {\n  const meta = document.querySelector(\n    'meta[name=\"initial-route\"]',\n  ) as HTMLMetaElement | null;\n  if (meta?.content?.trim()) {\n    return meta.content.trim();\n  }\n  const searchRoute = new URL(window.location.href).searchParams.get(\n    \"initialRoute\",\n  );\n  return searchRoute ? searchRoute.trim() : null;\n}\n\n/**\n * This will be set when an initial route meta tag is injected into the document.\n * When set, this webview should try to stay on the route it started on because when vs code reloads, it will reload this initial route.\n * Instead, when navigating, use the `navigate-in-new-editor-tab` message bus message to navigate to a new editor tab.\n */\nexport const initialRoute = getInitialRoute();\n"],"mappings":"AAAA,SAASA,GAAiC,CACxC,IAAMC,EAAOC,SAASC,cACpB,6BACD,CACD,GAAIF,GAAMI,SAASC,MAAM,CACvB,OAAOL,EAAKI,QAAQC,MAAM,CAE5B,IAAMC,EAAc,IAAIC,IAAIC,OAAOC,SAASC,KAAK,CAACC,aAAaC,IAC7D,eACD,CACD,OAAON,EAAcA,EAAYD,MAAM,CAAG,KAQ5C,IAAaQ,EAAed,GAAiB"}
C:\Users\ai\.vscode\extensions\openai.chatgpt-26.323.20928-win32-x64\webview\assets\use-navigate-to-local-conversation-D0E_a42x.js.map:1:{"version":3,"file":"use-navigate-to-local-conversation-D0E_a42x.js","names":["ThreadId","createConversationId","useCallback","useNavigate","initialRoute","messageBus","useNavigateToLocalConversation","$","_c","navigate","t0","conversationId","path","dispatchMessage"],"sources":["../../src/utils/use-navigate-to-local-conversation.ts"],"sourcesContent":["import type { ThreadId } from \"app-server-types\";\nimport { createConversationId } from \"protocol\";\nimport { useCallback } from \"react\";\nimport { useNavigate } from \"react-router\";\n\nimport { initialRoute } from \"@/initial-route-atom\";\nimport { messageBus } from \"@/message-bus\";\n\nexport function useNavigateToLocalConversation(): (\n  conversationId: ThreadId,\n) => void {\n  const navigate = useNavigate();\n\n  return useCallback(\n    (conversationId: ThreadId) => {\n      const path = `/local/${createConversationId(conversationId)}`;\n      if (initialRoute) {\n        // If there was an initial route for this webview, we don't want to navigate to a new conversation here because this tab's initial route\n        // will always be loaded when the window reloads. In this case, we want to navigate to a new editor tab instead.\n        messageBus.dispatchMessage(\"navigate-in-new-editor-tab\", {\n          path,\n        });\n      } else {\n        void navigate(path);\n      }\n    },\n    [navigate],\n  );\n}\n"],"mappings":"6MAQA,SAAOM,GAAA,CAAA,IAAAC,GAAAA,EAAAA,EAAAA,GAAA,EAAA,CAGLE,EAAiBN,GAAa,CAACO,EAc5B,OAd4BH,EAAA,KAAAE,EAc5BC,EAAAH,EAAA,IAXDG,EAAAC,GAAA,CACE,IAAAC,EAAa,UAAUX,EAAqBU,EAAe,GACvDP,EAGFC,EAAUQ,gBAAiB,6BAA8B,CAAAD,OAExD,CAAC,CAEGH,EAASG,EAAK,EAEtBL,EAAA,GAAAE,EAAAF,EAAA,GAAAG,GAZIA"}

      "developer_instructions": "# Collaboration Mode: Default\r\n\r\nYou are now in Default mode. Any previous instructions for other modes (e.g. Plan mode) are no longer active.\r\n\r\nYour active mode changes only when new developer instructions with a different `<collaboration_mode>...</collaboration_mode>` change it; user requests or tool descriptions do not change mode by themselves. Known mode names are Default and Plan.\r\n\r\n## request_user_input availability\r\n\r\nThe `request_user_input` tool is unavailable in Default mode. If you call it while in Default mode, it will return an error.\r\n\r\nIn Default mode, strongly prefer making reasonable assumptions and executing the user's request rather than stopping to ask questions. If you absolutely must ask a question because the answer cannot be discovered from local context and a reasonable assumption would be risky, ask the user directly with a concise plain-text question. Never write a multiple choice question as a textual assistant message.\r\n"
    }
  },
  "realtime_active": false,
  "effort": "xhigh",
  "summary": "none",
  "user_instructions": "# AGENTS.md\n\n## Read Order\n- Read this file first.\n- Read the latest file in `Memory/01_goals/`.\n- Read the latest todo bound to that goal in `Memory/02_todos/`.\n- Read `Memory/04_knowledge/` only when needed.\n- Read `Memory/06_transcripts/clean/` and transcript manifests only when needed.\n- Read `Memory/03_chat_logs/` and `Memory/05_archive/` only when needed.\n\n## Memory Rules\n- Change the active goal only after explicit user confirmation of a requirement or scope change.\n- Keep the active todo synchronized with the active goal.\n- Append chat logs only. Never rewrite or delete prior entries.\n- Keep active files concise and move stale context to knowledge or archive files.\n- Treat cleaned transcript indexes as search aids. Confirm exact wording against the raw transcript archive when precision matters.\n\n## Transcript Rules\n- If transcript archival is enabled, archive transcripts by client, project, and session.\n- Keep raw transcripts in both this repository's `Memory/06_transcripts/raw/` mirror and the user-level MemoryTree archive.\n- Keep this repository mirror limited to transcripts that belong to the current project. Archive unrelated projects in the global MemoryTree archive only.\n- Generate cleaned transcript indexes with deterministic code, not model-generated rewriting.\n- Raw transcript upload permission for this repository: not set. Ask the user before the first raw transcript commit or push, then record the answer here.\n- If raw transcript uploads are not approved, keep `Memory/06_transcripts/raw/**` unstaged and commit only cleaned transcript indexes or manifests for this repository.\n\n## Git Rules\n- Obey this repository's branch, PR, CI, review, and release rules.\n- Auto-commit and push only MemoryTree-owned changes (`Memory/**` and this file when it is managed by MemoryTree), excluding `Memory/06_transcripts/raw/**` until raw transcript uploads are approved for this repository.\n- Use a MemoryTree-scoped commit title. Prefer `memorytree(<scope>): <subject>`, or the repository-required equivalent such as `docs(memorytree): <subject>`.\n- Use a dedicated branch and PR for MemoryTree-only changes. Enable auto-merge only when repository rules permit it.\n- If a diff includes product code, shared policy files, cross-project transcript files, or unclear ownership, stop and ask the user before staging or pushing.\n- When `auto_push` is enabled, the heartbeat process pushes automatically after committing. If no remote is configured or a push fails, an alert is written to `~/.memorytree/alerts.json`.\n\n## Heartbeat Rules\n- Transcript discovery, import, cleaning, and push are handled by the background heartbeat process. The model does not execute these operations.\n- The model only writes chat log summaries and updates goals and todos during interactive sessions.\n- If `~/.memorytree/alerts.json` contains pending notifications, display them at the start of the session.\n- If `memorytree-daemon` is available but not registered on this machine, ask the user for their preferred settings and offer to run `memorytree-daemon install`.\n- When the user asks to see their most recent conversation, trigger an on-demand sync for the current project, locate the latest session across all clients (Claude Code, Codex, Gemini CLI), and generate a continuation summary.\n- All interactive prompts must use plain-text questions. Do not rely on client-specific UI components.\n",
  "truncation_policy": {
    "mode": "tokens",
    "limit": 10000
  }
}

Output:
---
name: memorytree-workflow
description: Detect, initialize, and maintain a reusable MemoryTree project-memory workspace for any code repository. On activation, immediately detect and report the repo's MemoryTree state.
---

# MemoryTree

Use this skill for repo-level project memory. Do not treat it as an application runtime feature unless the user explicitly asks to productize it.

## On Activation

When this skill is invoked, immediately perform these steps without waiting for further user input:

1. Detect the repo state (`not-installed`, `partial`, or `installed`) using `references/project-detection.md`.
2. Check `~/.memorytree/alerts.json`. If pending alerts exist, display them to the user before proceeding.
3. If `not-installed`: run `memorytree init` to scaffold the workspace, then report what was created.
4. If `partial`: run `memorytree upgrade` to add missing pieces, then report what was added.
5. If `installed`: read the active goal, bound todo, and latest chat log. Report the current state (goal summary, todo progress, pending tasks) and ask the user what they want to work on next. If `memorytree daemon status` shows the heartbeat is not registered, ask the user whether they want the recommended current-repository setup via `memorytree daemon quick-start --root <repo>` or only a machine-level scheduler restore via `memorytree daemon install --interval 5m --auto-push true`. Use `memorytree daemon register` only when they explicitly want custom branch, interval, report-port, or worktree settings. Use plain-text questions compatible with all AI assistants, not client-specific UI components.

Do not stop after detection to ask whether you should proceed. Act on the result immediately.

## Core Behavior

1. Detect the repo state before changing files. Use `references/project-detection.md`.
2. If MemoryTree is missing, initialize it with `memorytree init`. If the CLI is not available, create the files from `assets/templates/`.
3. If MemoryTree is partial, upgrade it with `memorytree upgrade` before manual maintenance.
4. If MemoryTree is already installed, maintain only the active goal, bound todo, and current chat log. Use `references/update-rules.md`.
5. Keep the installation compatible with the host repo's branch, PR, CI, and review rules. Use `references/git-policy.md`.
6. Prefer safe defaults: do not add reply suffix requirements unless the host repository already requires them, do not mix code-and-memory commits by default, and do not override branch policy. MemoryTree-only git automation is allowed only inside the isolated flow in `references/git-policy.md`.
7. When locale is not explicit, choose it from repo language first, then system language. Use `references/locale-selection.md`.
8. Keep reply language aligned with user language first, then repo locale. Use `references/response-language.md`.
9. For partial repos, use the non-destructive upgrade path before manual maintenance. Use `references/upgrade-path.md`.
10. When transcript archival is enabled, import transcripts with deterministic code, preserve raw transcripts as evidence in both the current repo mirror and the global archive, generate cleaned Markdown for indexing, keep cross-project backfills and ambiguous ownership cases in the global archive only, and ask once per repository whether raw transcript files may be committed or pushed. Use `references/transcript-archive.md`.
11. When CLI commands are needed, use `memorytree <subcommand>` (requires Node.js 鈮?20). If Node.js is not available, fall back to manual scaffolding from `assets/templates/`. See `references/execution-environment.md`.
12. Transcript discovery, import, cleaning, and push are handled by the background heartbeat process. The model only writes chat log summaries and updates goals and todos. See `references/heartbeat-scheduling.md`.
13. Global configuration is stored in `~/.memorytree/config.toml`. See `references/global-configuration.md`.
14. When the user asks to see their most recent conversation, trigger an on-demand transcript sync for the current project, locate the latest session across all three clients, and generate a continuation summary. See `references/transcript-archive.md` and `references/heartbeat-scheduling.md`.
15. All interactive prompts (heartbeat install, configuration, session continuity) must use plain-text questions compatible with every AI assistant that supports this skill. Do not rely on client-specific UI components. See `references/heartbeat-scheduling.md`.

## Read Order

1. Repo `AGENTS.md`, if present.
2. Latest file in `Memory/01_goals/`.
3. Latest todo bound to that goal in `Memory/02_todos/`.
4. `Memory/04_knowledge/` only when needed.
5. `Memory/06_transcripts/clean/`, transcript manifests, and repo-local raw transcript mirrors only when transcript history or cross-project memory search is relevant.
6. `Memory/03_chat_logs/` and `Memory/05_archive/` only when needed.

## Detect

1. Check whether `Memory/` exists and matches the layout in `references/memory-layout.md`.
2. Classify the repo as `not-installed`, `partial`, or `installed`.
3. If the repo already has stronger rules than the generated templates, preserve the repo rules and only add missing MemoryTree pieces.

## Upgrade

1. Use `memorytree upgrade` for repositories that already have `AGENTS.md`, custom policy files, or partial `Memory/` content.
2. Preserve existing repo policy files by default.
3. Create only the missing MemoryTree pieces.
4. Read the upgrade result and treat `agents_merge_required=true` as a manual review task, not an overwrite instruction.

## Initialize

1. Run `memorytree init --root <repo>` and pass `--project-name`, `--goal-summary`, and `--locale` when the user already provided them.
2. Use fresh init only when the repo does not already have `AGENTS.md` or another repo policy source such as a policy-bearing `CONTRIBUTING.md`, PR templates, `CODEOWNERS`, or commitlint config. If repo policy already exists, switch to `memorytree upgrade` instead of trying to skip `AGENTS.md`.
3. Review the generated `AGENTS.md` before keeping it. Merge it with the repo's existing policy if needed.
4. Keep the initial installation minimal: `Memory/01_goals`, `02_todos`, `03_chat_logs`, optional `04_knowledge`, `05_archive`, and `06_transcripts` only when transcript archival is enabled for the repo or needed for transcript import.
5. Use the locale-specific template files in `assets/templates/<locale>/` as the source of truth for first-run scaffolding.
6. Prefer `--locale auto` when the user did not request a language explicitly.
7. Use `memorytree locale` when you need a deterministic locale check without scaffolding files.
8. If Node.js is not available, create first-run files directly from `assets/templates/` instead of requiring a runtime installation.
9. If transcript archival is enabled for the repo, use `memorytree import --source <file>` for one explicit source file and `memorytree discover` when scanning local client stores. Matching transcripts must update both the repo mirror and the global archive, while unrelated projects, ambiguous ownership cases, or mismatched explicit sources are archived in the global archive only. Let the CLI generate cleaned transcripts deterministically without spending model tokens on the cleaning step.
10. Ask once whether raw transcript files may also be committed to the repository. Record the answer in `AGENTS.md`, keep the files in the repo either way, and exclude `raw` files from automatic staging until the user says yes.

## Maintain

1. Change the active goal only after explicit user confirmation of a scope or requirement change.
2. Update the active todo when progress changes, milestones move, or the next task becomes clearer.
3. Append chat logs only. Never rewrite or delete prior entries.
4. Keep active files small. Move stale context to archive or knowledge files instead of bloating the active goal or todo.
5. Treat product ideas, architecture decisions, and operating constraints as goal or knowledge content. Treat step-by-step execution state as todo content.
6. Treat cleaned transcript indexes as discovery aids, not as the final source of truth. When exact wording matters, confirm against the raw transcript archive.
7. Prefer deterministic CLI-based transcript cleaning over manual rewriting. Do not spend model tokens to restate or normalize transcript content when `memorytree import` can do it.
8. Reply in the user's language when clear; otherwise align with repo locale without rewriting existing files just to translate them.

## Git And Safety

1. Never assume MemoryTree can override the repo's branch model.
2. Default to isolated MemoryTree-only Git operations: stage only MemoryTree-owned files, use a MemoryTree-scoped commit title, push to a dedicated branch, and open a dedicated PR.
3. Enable auto-merge only for MemoryTree-only PRs, and only when the repository still enforces its required review and CI checks.
4. Keep repo-local raw transcript mirrors and the global transcript archive synchronized on every import. The global index must remain safe for concurrent multi-project updates.
5. Keep the repository mirror limited to transcripts that belong to the current repository. Unrelated or ambiguous transcripts belong in the global archive only.
6. Keep raw transcript files under `Memory/06_transcripts/raw/**` in the repo, but exclude them from automatic staging until the user explicitly approved raw transcript uploads for that repository.
7. If the diff mixes product code, shared policy files, cross-project transcript archives, or unclear ownership, stop and ask the user before committing or pushing.
8. If the repo has protected branches or PR-only rules, follow them even if older MemoryTree rules say otherwise.
9. When `auto_push` is enabled, the heartbeat process pushes automatically after committing. If no Git remote is configured, the push is skipped. If a push fails, the heartbeat retries once. In either case an alert is written to `~/.memorytree/alerts.json`. See `references/git-policy.md`.
10. During transcript cleaning, the heartbeat scans for sensitive information (API keys, passwords, tokens). Matches are logged as warnings only 鈥?no automatic deletion or redaction. See `references/heartbeat-scheduling.md`.

11. Do not modify the host repository's CI or E2E workflow definitions by default. If the user explicitly approves a MemoryTree-only optimization, prefer path-filtered or lightweight workflows for `Memory/**` and managed `AGENTS.md` PRs without weakening required branch protection.

## Resources

- `references/project-detection.md`: detect install state and choose init vs maintain.
- `references/memory-layout.md`: folder and file naming rules.
- `references/update-rules.md`: when to version goals, todos, and session logs.
- `references/git-policy.md`: safe Git defaults and policy merge guidance.
- `references/locale-selection.md`: locale choice rules and alias behavior.
- `references/response-language.md`: reply-language precedence and translation safety.
- `references/transcript-archive.md`: raw transcript archival, clean transcript indexing, per-client source rules, and repo upload confirmation rules.
- `references/upgrade-path.md`: safe adoption flow for partial repos.
- `references/execution-environment.md`: how to run MemoryTree CLI (requires Node.js 鈮?20).
- `references/heartbeat-scheduling.md`: background heartbeat architecture, daemon CLI, and execution flow.
- `references/global-configuration.md`: `~/.memorytree/` directory layout, `config.toml` schema, and `alerts.json` format.
- `assets/templates/en/` and `assets/templates/zh-cn/`: seed files for English and Simplified Chinese initialization.
- CLI commands: `memorytree init`, `memorytree upgrade`, `memorytree import`, `memorytree discover`, `memorytree locale`, `memorytree recall`, `memorytree daemon install|uninstall|run-once|watch|status|quick-start|register`.

Output:
# AGENTS.md

## Read Order
- Read this file first.
- Read the latest file in `Memory/01_goals/`.
- Read the latest todo bound to that goal in `Memory/02_todos/`.
- Read `Memory/04_knowledge/` only when needed.
- Read `Memory/06_transcripts/clean/` and transcript manifests only when needed.
- Read `Memory/03_chat_logs/` and `Memory/05_archive/` only when needed.

## Memory Rules
- Change the active goal only after explicit user confirmation of a requirement or scope change.
- Keep the active todo synchronized with the active goal.
- Append chat logs only. Never rewrite or delete prior entries.
- Keep active files concise and move stale context to knowledge or archive files.
- Treat cleaned transcript indexes as search aids. Confirm exact wording against the raw transcript archive when precision matters.

## Transcript Rules
- If transcript archival is enabled, archive transcripts by client, project, and session.
- Keep raw transcripts in both this repository's `Memory/06_transcripts/raw/` mirror and the user-level MemoryTree archive.
- Keep this repository mirror limited to transcripts that belong to the current project. Archive unrelated projects in the global MemoryTree archive only.
- Generate cleaned transcript indexes with deterministic code, not model-generated rewriting.
- Raw transcript upload permission for this repository: not set. Ask the user before the first raw transcript commit or push, then record the answer here.
- If raw transcript uploads are not approved, keep `Memory/06_transcripts/raw/**` unstaged and commit only cleaned transcript indexes or manifests for this repository.

## Git Rules
- Obey this repository's branch, PR, CI, review, and release rules.
- Auto-commit and push only MemoryTree-owned changes (`Memory/**` and this file when it is managed by MemoryTree), excluding `Memory/06_transcripts/raw/**` until raw transcript uploads are approved for this repository.
- Use a MemoryTree-scoped commit title. Prefer `memorytree(<scope>): <subject>`, or the repository-required equivalent such as `docs(memorytree): <subject>`.
- Use a dedicated branch and PR for MemoryTree-only changes. Enable auto-merge only when repository rules permit it.
- If a diff includes product code, shared policy files, cross-project transcript files, or unclear ownership, stop and ask the user before staging or pushing.
- When `auto_push` is enabled, the heartbeat process pushes automatically after committing. If no remote is configured or a push fails, an alert is written to `~/.memorytree/alerts.json`.

## Heartbeat Rules
- Transcript discovery, import, cleaning, and push are handled by the background heartbeat process. The model does not execute these operations.
- The model only writes chat log summaries and updates goals and todos during interactive sessions.
- If `~/.memorytree/alerts.json` contains pending notifications, display them at the start of the session.
- If `memorytree-daemon` is available but not registered on this machine, ask the user for their preferred settings and offer to run `memorytree-daemon install`.
- When the user asks to see their most recent conversation, trigger an on-demand sync for the current project, locate the latest session across all clients (Claude Code, Codex, Gemini CLI), and generate a continuation summary.
- All interactive prompts must use plain-text questions. Do not rely on client-specific UI components.

# Project Goal v003

## Metadata
- Version: v003
- Created: 2026-03-24
- Previous: goal_v002_20260319.md
- Updated: 2026-03-24

## North Star

OpenMnemo 搴斾粠鈥淎I 瀵硅瘽璁板繂搴曞骇鈥濊繘涓€姝ユ槑纭崌绾т负锛?

- 鍒涗綔缁撴灉

- 鍙浠绘剰涓婂眰浜у搧鍐嶆璋冪敤

鐨勯暱鏈熻祫浜т綋绯汇€?

## Core Definition

OpenMnemo 寮€婧愭牳蹇冪殑鑱岃矗涓嶆槸鐩存帴鍋氭垚鏌愪竴涓叿浣撲骇鍝侊紝鑰屾槸鎻愪緵涓€濂楃ǔ瀹氱殑璧勪骇鍐呮牳锛?

- 缁熶竴璧勪骇妯″瀷锛歚ProductAsset / MemoryAsset / SkillAsset`
- Git/Gitea 鍘熺敓瀛樺偍涓庣増鏈寲
- 璁板繂鏍戜笌鎺㈤拡寮忔绱?
- FTS + vector + graph + tree/probe 鐨勭粺涓€妫€绱㈠眰
- 缁熶竴瀵煎叆銆佹彁鐐笺€佸洖鍐欒兘鍔?
- 鏈€灏忚繍琛屾椂鎺ュ彛锛屼緵涓婂眰浜у搧璋冪敤

## Product Position

OpenMnemo 涓?deer-flow 涓€绫荤郴缁熺殑鍏崇郴锛屽畾浣嶄负锛?

- OpenMnemo锛氬垱閫犺祫浜у唴鏍?
- deer-flow 绫诲舰鎬侊細鍒涢€犳墽琛屽眰鎴栦笂灞備骇鍝佸舰鎬?

OpenMnemo 鏈潵鍙互琛嶇敓鍑哄绉嶄笂灞備骇鍝侊紝浣嗗紑婧愭牳蹇冩湰韬笉绛変簬鍏朵腑浠绘剰涓€绉嶄骇鍝併€?

## Open Source Core Boundary

### Must Include

- 璧勪骇瀵硅薄妯″瀷涓庡叧绯绘ā鍨?
- 瀵硅瘽銆佷唬鐮併€佹枃妗ｃ€佸伐浣滄祦缁撴灉鐨勭粺涓€瀵煎叆
- Git/Gitea 鍥炲啓涓庣増鏈寲
- 璁板繂鏍戣妭鐐圭敓鎴愩€佹敹鏁涖€佹帰閽堝睍寮€
- FTS / vector / graph / probe 缁熶竴妫€绱㈡帴鍙?
- 鎶€鑳藉璞″寲涓庢潵婧愯拷韪?
- 闈㈠悜涓婂眰浜у搧鐨勬渶灏忔暟鎹眰涓庤繍琛屾椂鎺ュ彛

### Must Not Rush Into Core

- 瀹屾暣 deer-flow 寮?super agent harness
- 閲嶅瀷澶?agent 缂栨帓 UI
- 浼佷笟绾ф潈闄愩€佸璁°€佽璐?
- 鎻掍欢甯傚満鎴?skill 甯傚満
- SaaS 鎵樼褰㈡€?
- 澶嶆潅鍟嗕笟鎺堟潈浣撶郴

## Near-term Direction

褰撳墠涓荤嚎搴斿湪宸叉湁鏁版嵁灞傝兘鍔涘熀纭€涓婄户缁帹杩涳紝浣嗙洰鏍囪浠庘€滆蹇嗗璞′笌妫€绱㈡敹鍙ｂ€濆崌绾т负鈥滃垱閫犺祫浜у唴鏍告敹鍙ｂ€濓細

1. 璁╃幇鏈?`memory_unit / source_asset / archive_anchor` 涓庝笁绫昏祫浜фā鍨嬪榻?
2. 鎶娾€滆蹇嗘爲 + 鎺㈤拡妫€绱⑩€濆崌涓烘寮忓鑸眰锛岃€屼笉鏄粎淇濈暀涓烘绱㈢瓥鐣ヨ鎯?
3. 鏄庣‘ `ProductAsset / MemoryAsset / SkillAsset` 鐨勬渶灏忓璞＄粨鏋勪笌鍏崇郴
4. 鍏堜互鈥滆祫浜ф姇褰?鑱氬悎灞傗€濇壙鎺ュ綋鍓?`DataLayerAPI` 涓庤繍琛屾椂瀵硅薄锛岃€屼笉鏄珛鍗崇敤鏂拌祫浜фā鍨嬫浛鎹?`session / memory_unit / source_asset / archive_anchor`
5. 璁╀笂灞?AI Chat銆亀orkflow銆乤gent銆亀orkspace 鏈潵閮介€氳繃璇ュ唴鏍稿洖鍐欒祫浜?

## Non-goals For Current Stage

- 涓嶅湪褰撳墠闃舵鎶?OpenMnemo 鐩存帴鍋氭垚瀹屾暣 deer-flow 鏇夸唬鍝?
- 涓嶅湪褰撳墠闃舵浼樺厛鎶曞叆澶嶆潅 UI 宸ヤ綔鍙?

## Success Signal

褰?OpenMnemo 寮€婧愭牳蹇冭兘澶熺ǔ瀹氬洖绛斾互涓嬮棶棰樻椂锛岃鏄庢湰杞畾浣嶅崌绾ф垚绔嬶細

- 涓€涓」鐩渶缁堜骇鍑轰簡鍝簺浜у搧璧勪骇锛?
- 杩欎簺璧勪骇鏄€氳繃鍝簺瀵硅瘽銆佸喅绛栥€佺害鏉熷拰閲岀▼纰戦€愭褰㈡垚鐨勶紵
- 杩欎簺杩囩▼鏈€缁堟矇娣€鍑轰簡鍝簺鍙鐢ㄦ妧鑳斤紵

# Project Todo

## Binding
- Goal version: goal_v003
- Todo version: v003_001
- Created: 2026-03-24
- Updated: 2026-03-25
- Status: active

## Current Focus
Stage gate confirmed on 2026-03-25: prioritize foundation work that makes memory invocation stable, precise, replayable, and traceable. Do not let super-agent orchestration displace this bottom-layer work before the substrate is proven.

鏈疆宸插畬鎴愨€滃垱閫犺祫浜у唴鏍糕€濊鍒掓敹鍙ｏ紝寮€濮嬭繘鍏ュ疄鐜扮骇 contract 钀藉湴鍑嗗闃舵锛屼紭鍏堢骇濡備笅锛?

- [x] 灏嗙幇鏈?`memory_unit / source_asset / archive_anchor` 涓?`ProductAsset / MemoryAsset / SkillAsset` 寤虹珛姝ｅ紡鏄犲皠
- [x] 鏄庣‘璁板繂鏍戞渶灏忚妭鐐瑰眰绾э細`ConversationLeaf / ArchiveBatch / MilestoneNode / StageNode`
- [x] 鏄庣‘鎺㈤拡寮忔绱㈡渶灏忔帴鍙ｏ細`probe_main(query, root_ref) / probe_branch(node_id, query?) / probe_leaf(node_id) / hydrate_raw`
- [x] 褰㈡垚 `Session Tree / Repo Tree / Global Memory Forest / Budget Profiles` 鐨勭粺涓€璁捐绋?
- [x] 灏嗙粺涓€璁捐绋挎敹鏁涗负瀹炵幇绾?schema銆乻torage mapping 涓?tool contract
- [x] 鏄庣‘涓夌被璧勪骇鐨勫叡浜熀绫诲瓧娈点€佺储寮曞瓧娈点€佸叧绯诲瓧娈点€佺増鏈瓧娈?
- [x] 鏄庣‘涓夌被璧勪骇妯″瀷涓庡綋鍓?`DataLayerAPI` 鐨勫吋瀹规ˉ锛岄伩鍏嶅舰鎴愪袱濂楀钩琛岃繍琛屾椂瀵硅薄

## Execution Notes
- Newly confirmed product rule: any capability that does not directly improve memory-call correctness, stability, traceability, or replayability should not move ahead of the substrate. Agent orchestration remains an upper-layer follow-on after the memory layer is dependable.

- 褰撳墠浠ｇ爜涓荤嚎浠嶄互鏃㈡湁鏁版嵁灞傛垚鏋滀负鍩虹锛屼笉鎺ㄧ炕宸插畬鎴愮殑 unit-first retrieval銆乴ocal runtime銆乵ixed retrieval銆?

- 鐜版湁瀹炵幇绾?contract 宸茬户缁ˉ鍒拌惤琛ㄧ骇鍜岃涓虹骇锛屼笅涓€姝ュ彲浠ョ洿鎺ユ寜 `tree_nodes / assets / asset_edges / root_refs / materialization_runs` 寮€濮嬬紪鐮併€?
- contract review 宸蹭慨姝ｄ袱涓叧閿偣锛歡enesis 璧勪骇鍏佽绌?`lineage`锛屽苟涓?`root_refs` / `asset_edges` 宸茶ˉ榻愬箓绛夌敓鎴愪笌鍞竴閿害鏉熴€?
- 璁板繂鍘嬬缉鏂规缁х画鎸夌収鈥滃叏閲忓師鏂囦繚鐣?+ 鏍戝舰绱㈠紩鏀舵暃 + 鎺㈤拡寮忛€愬眰灞曞紑鈥濆師鍒欐帹杩涖€?
- 涓夌被璧勪骇缁熶竴娌夋穩鍒扮敤鎴疯嚜鏈?Git/Gitea 浠撳簱涓紝淇濇寔 Git-native 瀹氫綅銆?

shell_command output=Exit code: 0 Wall time: 0.5 seconds Output: # 2026-03-25 12:51 Session Log --- Time: 2026-03-25 12:51 User: Asked whether OpenMnemo already has 鈥渉arness鈥?characteristics. Assist...

Output:
# 2026-03-25 12:51 Session Log

---
Time: 2026-03-25 12:51
User: Asked whether OpenMnemo already has 鈥渉arness鈥?characteristics.
Assistant: Reviewed the active goal and todo plus the README, CLI, sync, storage, chat, and data-layer code. Concluded that OpenMnemo already behaves like a memory and retrieval harness in several concrete ways: it has a unified CLI entry point, multi-client transcript discovery and import, adapter and factory abstractions for storage and chat providers, a heartbeat orchestrator, and a unified `DataLayerAPI` plus local runtime. At the same time, it is not yet a full super-agent harness because the codebase does not yet implement a general agent runtime, task planner or executor loop, tool registry, or multi-agent orchestration layer, and the active goal and todo explicitly defer that scope for the current stage.

---
Time: 2026-03-25 12:51
User: Confirmed that the project should keep improving the foundation first, and that agents can be orchestrated later once memory can be invoked with effectively unlimited accuracy.
Assistant: Confirmed this priority matches the current project direction and does not require a goal-version change. Synchronized the active todo by recording a stricter stage gate: the current mainline should prioritize memory-call correctness, stability, traceability, and replayability ahead of agent orchestration, and any feature that does not directly strengthen that foundation should not displace the bottom-layer work.

---
Time: 2026-03-25 13:08
User: Asked why `memorytree daemon quick-start --root .` produced no visible response in the `openmnemo` repository.
Assistant: Diagnosed that the command currently resolves to a zero-byte file at `C:\Users\ai\AppData\Local\Programs\Microsoft VS Code\memorytree`, so PowerShell launches a stub that exits silently. Verified that this repository's actual CLI command is `openmnemo`, defined in `packages/cli/package.json`, and that the supported daemon subcommands in the current code are only `install`, `uninstall`, `run-once`, `watch`, and `status` with no `quick-start` implementation. Confirmed `openmnemo daemon status` works and reports `Registered: no`, meaning the actionable next step is to use the working `openmnemo` CLI rather than the broken `memorytree` shim.

---
Time: 2026-03-25 13:21
User: Asked what the difference is between `openmnemo` and `memorytree`, and whether they refer to local folders or the current repository name.
Assistant: Clarified that `openmnemo` is the current repository and product/CLI name, while `MemoryTree` refers to the project-memory workflow, directory convention, and historical naming carried over from earlier tooling. Explained that the repo still contains some legacy `memorytree` wording in comments, AGENTS rules, and heartbeat labels, which is why the names appear mixed.

---
Time: 2026-03-25 13:31
User: Asked why the direct `memorytree` command is broken.
Assistant: Confirmed the breakage is environmental rather than repository code failure. Found that no proper global `memorytree` shim is installed under the normal npm bin path, while PowerShell instead resolves `memorytree` to a zero-byte file at `C:\Users\ai\AppData\Local\Programs\Microsoft VS Code\memorytree`. Also found similar zero-byte placeholder files such as `Calculate` and `閫愯缈昏瘧` in the same VS Code directory, suggesting a PATH pollution or placeholder-command mechanism outside this repository. Concluded that command lookup is being hijacked by an invalid file before the real MemoryTree CLI can run.

---
Time: 2026-03-25 13:36
User: Asked whether this should be considered a design flaw in the skill.
Assistant: Answered that the immediate failure is not caused by the `quick-start` implementation itself, but there is a real design and integration weakness around installation and invocation. The skill currently relies on a healthy `memorytree` command existing on PATH and does not proactively validate that the resolved executable is real, non-empty, and actually the intended CLI before telling the user to run it. Also noted that the coexistence of `memorytree` naming in the workflow tooling and `openmnemo` naming in the repository CLI increases confusion and makes the UX more fragile.

---
Time: 2026-03-25 13:33
User: Asked whether self-check would be enough to make the broken command usable.
Assistant: Clarified that self-check alone is diagnostic, not curative. It helps detect whether the resolved `memorytree` command is invalid and can improve UX by surfacing a clear error or switching to a fallback invocation, but the direct `memorytree` command only becomes usable immediately if self-check is paired with a fallback path or an automatic repair of the bad shim/PATH entry.

---
Time: 2026-03-25 13:35
User: Asked for a plain-language explanation of "self-check + fallback" versus "self-check + fix shim".
Assistant: Explained in simple terms that fallback means noticing the front door is broken and temporarily using the side door so the action can proceed right away, while fixing the shim means repairing the front door itself so future `memorytree ...` commands work normally without any workaround.

…252144 tokens truncated…ptor>;\n  restoreMessage: QueuedFollowUpMessage;\n};\n\nconst STEER_TURN_INACTIVE_ERROR_NAME = \"SteerTurnInactiveError\";\n\nexport async function steerTurn(\n  manager: AppServerManager,\n  conversationId: ConversationId,\n  params: SteerTurnRequestParams,\n): Promise<AppServer.v2.TurnSteerResponse> {\n  const role = manager.getStreamRole(conversationId);\n  const followerSteerTurnResponse = await manager.sendThreadFollowerRequest(\n    role,\n    \"thread-follower-steer-turn\",\n    {\n      conversationId,\n      input: params.input,\n      attachments: params.attachments,\n      restoreMessage: params.restoreMessage,\n    },\n  );\n  if (followerSteerTurnResponse) {\n    return followerSteerTurnResponse.result;\n  }\n\n  if (role?.role !== \"owner\") {\n    throw new Error(OWNER_WINDOW_ERROR);\n  }\n\n  if (!manager.isConversationStreaming(conversationId)) {\n    logger.error(\"Conversation is not being streamed.\", {\n      safe: { conversationId },\n      sensitive: {},\n    });\n    throw new Error(`Conversation ${conversationId} is not being streamed.`);\n  }\n\n  const conversationState = manager.getConversation(conversationId);\n  if (!conversationState) {\n    logger.error(\"Conversation state not found\", {\n      safe: { conversationId },\n      sensitive: {},\n    });\n    throw new Error(`Conversation state for ${conversationId} not found`);\n  }\n\n  const latestTurn = last(conversationState.turns);\n  if (latestTurn?.status !== \"inProgress\") {\n    throw createSteerTurnInactiveError(conversationId);\n  }\n\n  const pendingSteer = buildPendingSteerState(\n    uuidv4(),\n    params.input,\n    params.restoreMessage,\n    params.attachments,\n    latestTurn.turnId ?? null,\n    latestTurn.turnStartedAtMs ?? null,\n  );\n  manager.registerPendingSteer(conversationId, pendingSteer);\n\n  try {\n    const activeTurnId = await waitForActiveTurnId(manager, conversationId);\n    manager.setPendingSteerTurnId(\n      conversationId,\n      pendingSteer.id,\n      activeTurnId,\n    );\n    try {\n      return await sendSteerRequest(\n        manager,\n        conversationId,\n        params.input,\n        activeTurnId,\n      );\n    } catch (error) {\n      const updatedTurnId = getActiveTurnIdFromSteerConflict(error);\n      if (updatedTurnId == null) {\n        throw error;\n      }\n      manager.updateConversationState(conversationId, (draft) => {\n        const latestTurn = last(draft.turns);\n        if (latestTurn?.status !== \"inProgress\") {\n          return;\n        }\n        latestTurn.turnId = updatedTurnId;\n      });\n      manager.setPendingSteerTurnId(\n        conversationId,\n        pendingSteer.id,\n        updatedTurnId,\n      );\n      return await sendSteerRequest(\n        manager,\n        conversationId,\n        params.input,\n        updatedTurnId,\n      );\n    }\n  } catch (error) {\n    manager.removePendingSteer(conversationId, pendingSteer.id);\n    logger.error(\"Error submitting steering turn for conversation\", {\n      safe: { conversationId },\n      sensitive: { error },\n    });\n    throw error;\n  }\n}\n\nfunction createSteerTurnInactiveError(conversationId: ConversationId): Error {\n  const error = new Error(\n    `Cannot steer conversation ${conversationId} because its active turn already ended`,\n  );\n  error.name = STEER_TURN_INACTIVE_ERROR_NAME;\n  return error;\n}\n\nfunction createSteerTurnMissingActiveTurnIdError(\n  conversationId: ConversationId,\n): Error {\n  return new Error(\n    `Cannot steer conversation ${conversationId} without an active turn id`,\n  );\n}\n\nexport function isSteerTurnInactiveError(error: unknown): boolean {\n  return (\n    (error instanceof Error && error.name === STEER_TURN_INACTIVE_ERROR_NAME) ||\n    maybeErrorToString(error).includes(STEER_TURN_INACTIVE_ERROR_NAME)\n  );\n}\n\nfunction getActiveTurnId(\n  manager: AppServerManager,\n  conversationId: ConversationId,\n): string | null {\n  const conversationState = manager.getConversation(conversationId);\n  const latestTurn = last(conversationState?.turns ?? []);\n  if (latestTurn?.status !== \"inProgress\") {\n    return null;\n  }\n  return latestTurn.turnId ?? null;\n}\n\nasync function sendSteerRequest(\n  manager: AppServerManager,\n  conversationId: ConversationId,\n  input: Array<AppServer.v2.UserInput>,\n  expectedTurnId: string,\n): Promise<AppServer.v2.TurnSteerResponse> {\n  return await manager.sendRequest(\n    \"turn/steer\",\n    {\n      threadId: conversationId,\n      input,\n      expectedTurnId,\n    },\n    {\n      timeoutMs: DEFAULT_TIMEOUT_MS,\n    },\n  );\n}\n\nfunction getActiveTurnIdFromSteerConflict(error: unknown): string | null {\n  const message = maybeErrorToString(error);\n  const match = message.match(\n    /expected active turn id `[^`]+` but found `([^`]+)`/,\n  );\n  return match?.[1] ?? null;\n}\n\nasync function waitForActiveTurnId(\n  manager: AppServerManager,\n  conversationId: ConversationId,\n): Promise<string> {\n  // We can have an active turn before we have a turn id: startTurn() creates a\n  // local in-progress placeholder with turnId: null, then turn/started fills in\n  // the real id asynchronously. steerTurn() needs expectedTurnId, so wait for\n  // that id instead of failing fast in this window.\n  const activeTurnId = getActiveTurnId(manager, conversationId);\n  if (activeTurnId != null) {\n    return activeTurnId;\n  }\n\n  return await new Promise<string>((resolve, reject) => {\n    const rejectMissingTurn = (): void => {\n      reject(createSteerTurnMissingActiveTurnIdError(conversationId));\n    };\n    const timeout = window.setTimeout(() => {\n      unsubscribe();\n      rejectMissingTurn();\n    }, DEFAULT_TIMEOUT_MS);\n\n    const unsubscribe = manager.addConversationCallback(\n      conversationId,\n      (conversationState) => {\n        const latestTurn = last(conversationState.turns);\n        if (latestTurn?.status !== \"inProgress\") {\n          window.clearTimeout(timeout);\n          unsubscribe();\n          reject(createSteerTurnInactiveError(conversationId));\n          return;\n        }\n        if (latestTurn.turnId == null) {\n          return;\n        }\n        window.clearTimeout(timeout);\n        unsubscribe();\n        resolve(latestTurn.turnId);\n      },\n    );\n\n    const resolvedTurnId = getActiveTurnId(manager, conversationId);\n    if (resolvedTurnId != null) {\n      window.clearTimeout(timeout);\n      unsubscribe();\n      resolve(resolvedTurnId);\n    }\n  });\n}\n","export function normalizeManualThreadTitle(\n  rawTitle: string,\n  maxChars: number,\n): string | null {\n  const trimmed = rawTitle.trim().replace(/\\s+/g, \" \");\n  if (trimmed.length === 0) {\n    return null;\n  }\n\n  if (trimmed.length <= maxChars) {\n    return trimmed;\n  }\n\n  return `${trimmed.slice(0, maxChars - 1).trimEnd()}…`;\n}\n","import type * as AppServer from \"app-server-types\";\nimport { produce } from \"immer\";\nimport type { ConversationId } from \"protocol\";\n\nimport { extractPromptRequest } from \"@/prompts/render-prompt\";\nimport { logger } from \"@/utils/logger\";\nimport { fetchFromVSCode } from \"@/vscode-api\";\n\nimport type { AppServerManager } from \"../app-server-manager\";\nimport {\n  MANUAL_THREAD_TITLE_MAX_CHARS,\n  TITLE_INPUT_CHAR_LIMIT,\n} from \"../app-server-manager-constants\";\nimport { normalizeManualThreadTitle } from \"../utils/normalize-manual-thread-title\";\n\nexport async function generateConversationTitle(\n  manager: AppServerManager,\n  conversationId: ConversationId,\n  input: Array<AppServer.v2.UserInput>,\n  cwd: string | null,\n): Promise<void> {\n  const conversation = manager.getConversation(conversationId);\n  if (!conversation || conversation.title) {\n    return;\n  }\n\n  const promptText = input\n    .filter(\n      (item): item is Extract<AppServer.v2.UserInput, { type: \"text\" }> =>\n        item.type === \"text\",\n    )\n    .map((item) => item.text)\n    .join(\"\")\n    .trim();\n  const extractedPrompt = extractPromptRequest(promptText).trim();\n  if (extractedPrompt.length === 0) {\n    return;\n  }\n  const trimmedPrompt =\n    extractedPrompt.length > TITLE_INPUT_CHAR_LIMIT\n      ? extractedPrompt.slice(0, TITLE_INPUT_CHAR_LIMIT)\n      : extractedPrompt;\n\n  try {\n    const response = await fetchFromVSCode(\"generate-thread-title\", {\n      params: {\n        hostId: manager.getHostId(),\n        prompt: trimmedPrompt,\n        cwd,\n      },\n    });\n    const title = response.title?.trim() ?? \"\";\n    if (title.length === 0) {\n      return;\n    }\n    manager.updateConversationState(conversationId, (draft) => {\n      if (!draft.title) {\n        draft.title = title;\n      }\n    });\n    await persistThreadTitle(manager, { conversationId, title });\n  } catch (error) {\n    logger.warning(\"Failed to generate thread title\", {\n      safe: { conversationId },\n      sensitive: {\n        conversationId,\n        error: error,\n      },\n    });\n  }\n}\n\nasync function persistThreadTitle(\n  manager: AppServerManager,\n  {\n    conversationId,\n    title,\n  }: {\n    conversationId: ConversationId;\n    title: string;\n  },\n): Promise<void> {\n  // TODO: Check for thread title duplication before setting it.\n  try {\n    await manager.sendRequest(\"thread/name/set\", {\n      threadId: conversationId,\n      name: title,\n    });\n  } catch (error) {\n    logger.warning(`Failed to set thread title`, {\n      safe: { conversationId },\n      sensitive: {\n        error: error,\n      },\n    });\n  }\n}\n\nexport async function setThreadTitle(\n  manager: AppServerManager,\n  conversationId: ConversationId,\n  title: string,\n): Promise<void> {\n  const normalized = normalizeManualThreadTitle(\n    title,\n    MANUAL_THREAD_TITLE_MAX_CHARS,\n  );\n  const nextTitle = normalized ?? null;\n  if (!nextTitle) {\n    return;\n  }\n  const conversation = manager.getConversation(conversationId);\n  if (conversation) {\n    const nextState = produce(conversation, (draft) => {\n      draft.title = nextTitle;\n    });\n    manager.applyThreadTitleStateUpdateAndNotify(nextState);\n  }\n  await persistThreadTitle(manager, { conversationId, title: nextTitle });\n}\n\nexport async function renameConversationTitle(\n  manager: AppServerManager,\n  conversationId: ConversationId,\n  title: string,\n): Promise<void> {\n  const normalized = normalizeManualThreadTitle(\n    title,\n    MANUAL_THREAD_TITLE_MAX_CHARS,\n  );\n  const nextTitle = normalized ?? null;\n  if (!nextTitle) {\n    return;\n  }\n  const conversation = manager.getConversation(conversationId);\n  if (!conversation) {\n    return;\n  }\n  const nextState = produce(conversation, (draft) => {\n    draft.title = nextTitle;\n  });\n  manager.applyThreadTitleStateUpdateAndNotify(nextState);\n  await persistThreadTitle(manager, { conversationId, title: nextTitle });\n}\n","import type { ConversationId } from \"protocol\";\n\nimport { logger } from \"@/utils/logger\";\n\nimport type { AppServerManager } from \"../app-server-manager\";\n\nexport async function updateThreadGitBranch(\n  manager: AppServerManager,\n  conversationId: ConversationId,\n  branch: string,\n): Promise<boolean> {\n  const nextBranch = branch.trim();\n  if (nextBranch.length === 0) {\n    return false;\n  }\n\n  try {\n    // Do not short-circuit on in-memory gitInfo.branch here. Worktree\n    // conversations can mirror the live branch into memory before the\n    // persisted thread metadata has been updated.\n    await manager.sendRequest(\"thread/metadata/update\", {\n      threadId: String(conversationId),\n      gitInfo: {\n        branch: nextBranch,\n      },\n    });\n    manager.updateConversationState(conversationId, (draft) => {\n      draft.gitInfo = {\n        branch: nextBranch,\n        sha: draft.gitInfo?.sha ?? null,\n        originUrl: draft.gitInfo?.originUrl ?? null,\n      };\n    });\n    return true;\n  } catch (error) {\n    logger.warning(\"Failed to update thread git branch metadata\", {\n      safe: {\n        conversationId,\n      },\n      sensitive: {\n        error,\n      },\n    });\n    return false;\n  }\n}\n","import type * as AppServer from \"app-server-types\";\nimport { z } from \"zod\";\n\nconst READ_THREAD_TERMINAL_ARGUMENTS_SCHEMA = z.strictObject({});\nconst NO_TERMINAL_SESSION_TEXT =\n  \"No app terminal session is attached to this thread yet.\";\n\nexport type ThreadTerminalSnapshot = {\n  cwd: string;\n  shell: string;\n  buffer: string;\n  truncated: boolean;\n};\n\nexport const READ_THREAD_TERMINAL_TOOL_NAME = \"read_thread_terminal\";\nexport const READ_THREAD_TERMINAL_TOOL: AppServer.v2.DynamicToolSpec = {\n  name: READ_THREAD_TERMINAL_TOOL_NAME,\n  description:\n    \"Read the current app terminal output for this desktop thread. Use it when you need shell output or the current prompt before deciding the next step. This tool takes no arguments.\",\n  inputSchema: {\n    type: \"object\",\n    properties: {},\n    additionalProperties: false,\n  },\n};\n\nexport function buildDynamicToolFailureResponse(\n  text: string,\n): AppServer.v2.DynamicToolCallResponse {\n  return {\n    contentItems: [\n      {\n        type: \"inputText\",\n        text,\n      },\n    ],\n    success: false,\n  };\n}\n\nexport function validateReadThreadTerminalToolArguments(\n  argumentsValue: unknown,\n): string | null {\n  const parsed =\n    READ_THREAD_TERMINAL_ARGUMENTS_SCHEMA.safeParse(argumentsValue);\n  if (parsed.success) {\n    return null;\n  }\n  return `${READ_THREAD_TERMINAL_TOOL_NAME} takes no arguments.`;\n}\n\nexport function hasReadableThreadTerminalSnapshot(\n  session: ThreadTerminalSnapshot | null,\n): boolean {\n  if (session == null) {\n    return false;\n  }\n\n  return (\n    session.cwd.length > 0 ||\n    session.shell !== \"unknown\" ||\n    session.buffer.length > 0 ||\n    session.truncated\n  );\n}\n\nexport function buildReadThreadTerminalToolResponse(\n  session: ThreadTerminalSnapshot | null,\n): AppServer.v2.DynamicToolCallResponse {\n  if (session == null) {\n    return buildDynamicToolFailureResponse(NO_TERMINAL_SESSION_TEXT);\n  }\n\n  const lines = [\n    \"App terminal snapshot for this thread:\",\n    `cwd: ${session.cwd}`,\n    `shell: ${session.shell}`,\n    session.truncated\n      ? \"note: output is truncated to the latest terminal buffer kept by the app.\"\n      : null,\n    \"```text\",\n    session.buffer.length > 0 ? session.buffer : \"[terminal has no output yet]\",\n    \"```\",\n  ].filter((line): line is string => line != null);\n\n  return {\n    contentItems: [\n      {\n        type: \"inputText\",\n        text: lines.join(\"\\n\"),\n      },\n    ],\n    success: true,\n  };\n}\n","import type { ConversationId, QueuedFollowUpMessage } from \"protocol\";\n\nimport { messageBus } from \"@/message-bus\";\n\nexport function broadcastQueuedFollowUpsChanged(\n  conversationId: ConversationId,\n  messages: Array<QueuedFollowUpMessage>,\n): void {\n  messageBus.dispatchMessage(\"thread-queued-followups-changed\", {\n    conversationId,\n    messages,\n  });\n}\n","import type * as AppServer from \"app-server-types\";\nimport type { WritableDraft } from \"immer\";\nimport type { ConversationId, McpRequestId } from \"protocol\";\n\nimport { logger } from \"@/utils/logger\";\n\nimport type { ApprovalActionContext } from \"../app-server-approval-actions\";\nimport type {\n  AppServerConversationState,\n  StreamRole,\n} from \"../app-server-manager\";\nimport { isPlanImplementationRequest } from \"../conversation-request\";\n\nexport function buildApprovalActionContext(params: {\n  hostId: string;\n  getStreamRole: (conversationId: ConversationId) => StreamRole | null;\n  conversations: Map<ConversationId, AppServerConversationState>;\n  updateConversationState: (\n    conversationId: ConversationId,\n    updater: (draft: WritableDraft<AppServerConversationState>) => void,\n  ) => void;\n  upsertUserInputResponseSyntheticItem: (\n    draft: WritableDraft<AppServerConversationState>,\n    requestId: McpRequestId,\n    requestParams: Extract<\n      AppServer.ServerRequest,\n      { method: \"item/tool/requestUserInput\" }\n    >[\"params\"],\n    answersByQuestionId: Record<string, Array<string>>,\n    completed: boolean,\n  ) => void;\n  upsertMcpServerElicitationSyntheticItem: (\n    draft: WritableDraft<AppServerConversationState>,\n    requestId: McpRequestId,\n    requestParams: Extract<\n      AppServer.ServerRequest,\n      { method: \"mcpServer/elicitation/request\" }\n    >[\"params\"],\n    completed: boolean,\n    action: AppServer.v2.McpServerElicitationRequestResponse[\"action\"] | null,\n  ) => void;\n}): ApprovalActionContext {\n  return {\n    hostId: params.hostId,\n    getStreamRole: params.getStreamRole,\n    getConversationRequest: (\n      conversationId,\n      requestId,\n    ): AppServer.ServerRequest | null => {\n      const role = params.getStreamRole(conversationId);\n      if (role?.role === \"follower\") {\n        throw new Error(\n          \"Please continue this conversation on the window where it was started.\",\n        );\n      }\n\n      const existingState = params.conversations.get(conversationId);\n      if (!existingState) {\n        logger.error(\"Conversation state not found\", {\n          safe: { conversationId },\n          sensitive: {},\n        });\n        return null;\n      }\n      const request = existingState.requests.find(\n        (candidate): candidate is AppServer.ServerRequest =>\n          candidate.id === requestId && !isPlanImplementationRequest(candidate),\n      );\n      if (!request) {\n        logger.error(\"Request not found\", {\n          safe: { requestId },\n          sensitive: {},\n        });\n        return null;\n      }\n\n      return request;\n    },\n    removeConversationRequest: (conversationId, requestId): void => {\n      params.updateConversationState(conversationId, (draft) => {\n        draft.requests = draft.requests.filter(\n          (request) => request.id !== requestId,\n        );\n      });\n    },\n    applyUserInputResponse: (\n      conversationId,\n      requestId,\n      requestParams,\n      answersByQuestionId,\n    ): void => {\n      params.updateConversationState(conversationId, (draft) => {\n        params.upsertUserInputResponseSyntheticItem(\n          draft,\n          requestId,\n          requestParams,\n          answersByQuestionId,\n          true,\n        );\n        draft.requests = draft.requests.filter(\n          (request) => request.id !== requestId,\n        );\n      });\n    },\n    applyMcpServerElicitationResponse: (\n      conversationId,\n      requestId,\n      requestParams,\n      action,\n    ): void => {\n      params.updateConversationState(conversationId, (draft) => {\n        params.upsertMcpServerElicitationSyntheticItem(\n          draft,\n          requestId,\n          requestParams,\n          true,\n          action,\n        );\n        draft.requests = draft.requests.filter(\n          (request) => request.id !== requestId,\n        );\n      });\n    },\n  };\n}\n","import type * as AppServer from \"app-server-types\";\n\nimport { extractPromptRequest } from \"@/prompts/render-prompt\";\n\nconst DEFAULT_THREAD_NAME = \"Codex Task\";\n\nexport function buildWorktreeLabelFromInput(\n  input: Array<AppServer.v2.UserInput>,\n): string {\n  const textItem = input.find((item) => item.type === \"text\");\n  if (!textItem) {\n    return DEFAULT_THREAD_NAME;\n  }\n  const extracted = extractPromptRequest(textItem.text).trim();\n  if (extracted.length === 0) {\n    return DEFAULT_THREAD_NAME;\n  }\n  const normalized = extracted.replace(/\\s+/g, \" \").trim();\n  if (normalized.length === 0) {\n    return DEFAULT_THREAD_NAME;\n  } else if (normalized.length <= 80) {\n    return normalized;\n  }\n  return `${normalized.slice(0, 79).trimEnd()}…`;\n}\n","import type * as AppServer f
